text.title: OpenAI Assistants API — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `openai-assistants` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Treat the Assistant as a long-lived resource — create once at deploy, version explicitly, never inline-create per request.
rule.rationale: Per-request creation leaks $$ and breaks deterministic behaviour because instructions can drift between requests.

rule.statement: Each user session gets one thread; thread id is persisted server-side with a TTL. On session end the thread is deleted.
rule.rationale: Threads not deleted accumulate forever; OpenAI charges nothing per thread but File Search vector stores attached to them charge $0.10/GB/day.

rule.statement: When File Search is enabled, vector stores are named, versioned, and tied to a TTL. Anonymous vector stores are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unnamed vector stores are impossible to audit and pile up; named ones can be diffed and pruned.

rule.statement: Run polling is bounded — max 60s wait or stream-mode events. Polling without timeout is rejected.
rule.rationale: Stuck runs (rare but happen) cause the worker thread to spin until OOM; bounded waits convert hang into a controlled timeout.

rule.statement: Every assistant call records (assistant_id, thread_id, tokens, $cost) to telemetry. Anonymous spend is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without per-assistant telemetry, cost spikes are invisible until the monthly invoice lands.
